title: Here are the richest people in Massachusetts, according to Forbes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Nearly 30 people with ties to Massachusetts are billionaires, according to Forbes' 2026 World’s Billionaires List.
William "Bill" Chisholm, Boston Celtics owner, and Larry Culp, CEO of GE Aerospace, are new Massachusetts billionaires.
Abigail Johnson, CEO of Fidelity Investments, is the richest person in Massachusetts with a net worth of $33.2 billion.

title: ‘I am a Raider’: Maxx Crosby breaks silence after Ravens trade falls through
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maxx Crosby addressed fans on social media after a trade to the Baltimore Ravens fell through due to medical concerns.
Crosby, who has spent seven years with the Raiders, stated 'I am a Raider' and reaffirmed his commitment to the team.
The Patriots are considering Crosby to bolster their pass-rushing, but concerns about his medicals and draft capital remain.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her grandmother, support cancer patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Laura Davis from Wakefield, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon in memory of her grandmother 'Gamma'.
Her grandmother, a significant figure in her life, passed away from Stage 4 pancreatic cancer in 2015.
Davis has completed five marathons to raise money for charitable causes, including four for cancer support.

title: Carjacking suspect shot and killed in Roxbury, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston police shot and killed a carjacking suspect in Roxbury after he accelerated a stolen vehicle towards them.
The incident occurred around 9:45 p.m. near 10 Linwood Square, following a carjacking report at 1500 Tremont St.
The suspect was taken to a hospital and pronounced dead; involved officers were also hospitalized but are in good condition.

title: Colin Dorgan, who lost 3 family members in Pawtucket shooting, scores game-winning goal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Colin Dorgan scored the game-winning goal for Blackstone Valley Co-op in double overtime against Portsmouth.
Dorgan's mother, brother, and grandfather were killed in a February shooting at Dennis M. Lynch Arena.
Colin's team supports him with patches commemorating his family as they advance to the Rhode Island Division 2 finals.

title: ‘We are in the midst of winter versus spring here’: Ski resorts are closing operations, reducing lift prices, limiting lifts
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Massachusetts and Vermont ski areas closed operations due to severe weather on Thursday.
Wildcat Mountain in New Hampshire adjusted lift prices and operations amid warm weather and rain.
Jay Peak in Vermont limited lifts due to high winds and expected trail freeze-up.

title: Two officers rescue woman found asleep amid house fire in Merrimac
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two Merrimac police officers rescued a woman found asleep during a house fire early Wednesday morning.
Jeff Strogney, a resident, called 911 after seeing 10-15 foot flames and smoke from the home.
Officers Paul DeCoste and Adam White entered the home, waking the woman and confirming she was the only occupant.

title: A 22-year-old man died in a three-car crash in Canton
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 22-year-old man from Marshfield, identified as Mathew McTiernan, died in a car crash on I-95 in Canton.
The crash involved three cars and occurred at 6:20 a.m., affecting two of the three traffic lanes.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating the cause of the crash, which was responded to by troopers from the Milton Barracks.

title: Baby boomers feeling crushed by housing crunch
author: Lindsay Crudele
bullets: Baby boomers represented 53% of home sellers and 42% of buyers in 2025, facing housing instability as they age.
Shorelight Real Estate raised rents by up to 70% at a Southbridge senior complex, prompting affordability concerns for fixed-income residents.
Massachusetts sees increasing senior housing demand; 20% of baby boomers lack savings, affecting long-term housing stability.

title: You can now pass through TSA PreCheck at Logan using only your face. Should that make us nervous?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Logan Airport launched a Touchless ID program allowing TSA PreCheck flyers to verify identity using facial recognition technology.
TSA says the system enhances security and efficiency, but privacy advocates warn about normalization of mass surveillance.
Travelers can opt-out of facial recognition, with data reportedly deleted within 24 hours of departure to protect privacy.
